items.title: UK Borrowing Overshoots in Warning to New Burnham Government
items.description: Britain borrowed more than forecast in the first three months of the fiscal year, a reminder to new Chancellor of the Exchequer John Healey of the challenges he faces to keep the public finances on tr

items.title: FTSE 100 Live: Pound Steady After Healey Named Chancellor
Merz Gets a Chance to Reset His Chancellorship After Ally Quits
items.description: The 48-hour firestorm that took down one of German Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s key parliamentary allies has given him a window to tighten his grip on the policymaking apparatus.

items.title: Palm Slips from Month-High as Weak Crude Dents Biofuel Appeal
items.description: Palm oil fell from a near one-month high following weakness in the crude and vegetable oil complex, denting the appeal of biofuels.

items.title: Turkish Airlines Exploring Acquisitions in Asia, South America
items.description: Turkish Airlines is exploring more acquisition opportunities as it looks to expand its global reach.

items.title: Japan Insurers Buy Most Super-Long Bonds in Three Years in June
items.description: Japanese insurance firms bought the most super-long government bonds in three years last month, adding to signs that demand from a key buyer is stabilizing.

items.title: Iron Ore Drops as China Steel Losses Deepen, Hormuz Risks Rise
items.description: Iron ore fell for a second day as deteriorating profit margins at Chinese steel mills and continued US-Iran hostilities clouded the demand outlook.

items.title: Goldman Says Brent Could Top $120 If Hormuz Disruptions Persist
items.description: Brent could rally to more than $120 a barrel by the fourth quarter if disruptions of the Strait of Hormuz persist, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc., although that’s not the bank’s base case.

items.title: Global Investors Turn Cautious on Indian Bonds
items.description: The rupee is also under strain, with slowing debt inflows coinciding with renewed foreign selling in stocks.

items.title: China Unleashes Broad State Support to Halt Tech Stock Selloff
items.description: China is mounting one of its broadest efforts in years to steady the stock market, with regulators, state-backed investors, insurers and asset managers all moving to shore up confidence after a sellof

items.title: SBI Funds Rises 8.5% in Debut After $1 Billion India IPO
items.description: SBI Funds Management Ltd. shares rose 8.5% in their Mumbai debut Tuesday after investors piled into the company’s $1 billion initial public offering, making it one of India’s most heavily subscribed b

items.title: Moody’s Flags Rising Risks in Indonesia on Policy Uncertainty
items.description: Moody’s Ratings remains concerned about policy uncertainty and fiscal sustainability risks in Indonesia, reinforcing its cautious outlook and warning that downside risks are likely to persist.

items.title: Korean Stock Traders Slash Margin Loans to Lowest Since April
items.description: South Korean investors slashed their leveraged stock positions to the lowest level in three months, as memory-chip losses halted the market’s rally.

items.title: Global Stocks Lift Australian Pension Funds to 9.5% Annual Gain
items.description: A rally in global equities delivered another strong year for Australia’s A$4.4 trillion ($3.1 trillion) pension fund industry, despite lingering concerns over the Middle East conflict and stubborn inf

items.title: Australia’s Stock Market Emerges as Haven From Volatile AI Trade
items.description: Australia’s stock market has emerged as an unlikely haven from the artificial intelligence-driven volatility that has roiled Asian equities in recent months.

items.title: Gold Rises on Dip-Buying as Traders Weigh Middle East Clashes
items.description: Gold rose on dip-buying as traders monitored developments in the Middle East for clues on the energy price impact on inflation.

items.title: New Zealand’s Faster Inflation Bolsters Case for More Rate Hikes
items.description: New Zealand’s annual inflation accelerated to the fastest pace in more than two years, fanned by soaring fuel prices and bolstering the case for the Reserve Bank to execute back-to-back interest-rate

items.title: Kioxia Bulls Ride Out Volatility With Targets 130% Above Market
items.description: Analysts are maintaining bullish views on Kioxia Holdings Inc. despite a deepening share price correction, saying the stock is likely to resume its upward trajectory once supply-demand conditions impr

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 21
items.description: Brent oil slipped after surging almost 6% over the previous two sessions, as traders weighed continued hostilities between the US and Iran against efforts to broker a new ceasefire.

items.title: Stocks Rise as Chipmakers Extend Rebound, Oil Dips: Markets Wrap
items.description: Asian stocks rose for the first time in four days as investors returned to chipmakers, driving a rebound in the sector after the recent selloff. Oil declined.
